response.justification: The candidate brings genuine breadth, current Staff-level experience at a major fintech with hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work, and real high-throughput systems exposure from low-latency ad-tech microservices handling millions of requests per second. However, Python — a must-have — is conspicuously absent from both the skills list and every role description, the distributed-systems depth dates largely to 2018–2020 in DLang rather than recent mainstream stacks, and there is little concrete evidence of directing technical strategy, managing cross-functional stakeholders, or operational/on-call ownership at staff scope. Strong enough on the AI-native and TypeScript dimensions to warrant a conversation, but with clear gaps against the must-have list.
strengths: Current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow experience at a top-tier engineering organization
Proven high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js depth plus unusual breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and open source
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume despite it being a must-have skill
Little demonstrated experience directing technical strategy or managing stakeholders at staff scope
Core distributed-systems experience is several years old and in a niche language (DLang)
